triggerPath: Operator presses Pause during an active burn move.
failureMode: The app sends GRBL feed hold only and marks the streamer paused without sending laser-off/reset or verifying laser state.
consequence: Beam behavior during hold is not proven safe; GRBL feed hold is not a guaranteed spindle/laser-off command.
evidence: RT_HOLD is defined as '!'.
pauseJob sends only RT_HOLD.
GRBL docs say feed hold does not disable spindle/coolant.
LightBurn docs distinguish Pause/Stop and require physical emergency stop readiness.
concreteFix: Write failing pause safety tests, then either label Pause as non-safety hold with UI warning or change Pause to a safety-first stop/reset policy with hardware verification.
notes: This should move ahead of static parity fixes because it affects live laser behavior.

failureMode: Nearest-neighbor ordering can cut outer contours before inner contours.
consequence: Parts can shift or drop before internal details are cut.
evidence: optimizePaths implements greedy nearest-neighbor ordering.
No polygon containment or depth ordering was found.
LightBurn documents Cut Inner Shapes First as an optimization setting.
concreteFix: Add containment depth ordering for closed cut paths before nearest-neighbor optimization, with tests for nested squares, holes, open paths, and mixed groups.
notes: This is the cleanest pure-core parity fix after pause safety.

triggerPath: Future output strategy or regression emits travel move with nonzero S value.
failureMode: Laser-off travel predicate exists but is not wired into preflight/output gating.
consequence: A future G-code regression could pass app preflight without catching laser-on travel.
evidence: findLaserOnTravelIssues is defined.
runPreflight imports bounds checking but not the laser-on-travel predicate.
Current GRBL strategy emits S0 on G0, so this is defense-in-depth rather than proof current output is unsafe.
concreteFix: Wire the predicate into preflight or emitGcode and add a failing test with intentionally unsafe G-code.
notes: Pair with existing emitter behavior instead of replacing it.

triggerPath: User frames or starts a job expecting center/corner/current-position origin behavior.
failureMode: Core has 9-anchor types, but UI/start readiness uses hardcoded front-left placement.
consequence: Job may start from an unexpected physical location.
evidence: JobOriginAnchor includes 9 positions.
Default placement constants use front-left.
LightBurn exposes Start From modes and a 9-dot Job Origin selector.
concreteFix: Expose absolute/user-origin mode plus 9-dot anchor UI first; handle Current Position and Stored Position in a separate state-safety ADR.
notes: Use existing core math rather than inventing new placement math.

triggerPath: Image import/engrave with logo, photo, or small text.
failureMode: Raster engrave path exposes only threshold, Floyd-Steinberg, and grayscale, while trace has more kernels and LightBurn exposes broader image controls.
consequence: Engraving output can be coarse or mismatched against LightBurn expectations.
evidence: Raster dither type has three modes.
Trace dither path has a broader kernel set.
LightBurn Image Mode docs list multiple modes and image controls.
concreteFix: Promote deterministic trace kernels into raster S-value output where appropriate, then add schema/UI/golden tests and one hardware burn comparison.
notes: Do not blindly expose every trace mode until raster output semantics are proven.

triggerPath: User wants one cut or engrave layer to run before another.
failureMode: Layer parameters can be edited, but layer order/priority cannot be changed from the UI.
consequence: Output order is import/internal order rather than operator-selected order.
evidence: setLayerParam updates layer settings, not order.
CutsLayersPanel maps layers without reorder controls.
LightBurn supports Order By Layer and Order By Priority.
concreteFix: Add moveLayer state action, UI up/down controls, and compile-order tests; add priority only if needed after layer order.
notes: Layer order is smaller and more testable than full LightBurn priority behavior.

triggerPath: User expects LightBurn-like Min Power or M3/M4 control.
failureMode: Single-power layer model and fixed modal output do not represent LightBurn's Max Power/Min Power/firmware support distinction.
consequence: A naive Min Power field could mislead users and produce unexpected output on GRBL firmware.
evidence: Layer model has one main power value.
LightBurn Shared Settings document Max Power and Min Power.
Firmware behavior depends on GRBL laser mode and modal command policy.
concreteFix: Write an ADR for Min Power, M3/M4, and GRBL $31 assumptions before adding schema/UI/output behavior.
notes: This is a design decision, not a quick UI field.

triggerPath: Import SVG using physical units, defs, symbols, or use references.
failureMode: Current parser handles recursive transforms, but remaining gaps include unit-aware scaling and use/symbol expansion.
consequence: Some SVGs import at wrong scale or omit reused symbol content.
evidence: parseTransform and presentationStateFor exist.
Width/height use Number.parseFloat, dropping units.
No confirmed use/href/symbol expansion was found.
concreteFix: Add failing tests for mm/cm/in/px/viewBox and defs/symbol/use, then implement only those cases.
notes: Reject the stale no-transform claim; keep the residual import-fidelity finding.

failureMode: Original audit claimed raster ETA undercount because planner skips raster groups.
consequence: Do not spend implementation time on this as a current defect unless fresh tests fail.
evidence: planner.ts skips raw raster groups.
estimateJobDuration wraps jobs with jobWithRasterSweeps before estimating.
Raster duration tests exist.
concreteFix: Keep tests and add later hardware calibration; no Tier 0 code change from the stale claim.
notes: This is a correction to the generated audit, not a confirmed open defect.

triggerPath: User expects basic drawing or advanced LightBurn editing operations.
failureMode: Primitive shape creation is absent; advanced operations are outside current project scope.
consequence: Users must use external tools for layout/editing, but implementing all LightBurn editing would explode scope.
evidence: Scene object union has imported SVG, text, traced image, and raster image rather than primitives.
Toolbar lacks primitive drawing tools.
Project docs mark boolean ops, node editing, kerf/offset, tabs/bridges, camera, rotary, DXF/PDF, and variable text out of scope.
concreteFix: If approved, add primitive parametric shapes and grouping first. Keep advanced operations behind explicit PROJECT/ADR scope changes.
notes: This is a real parity gap but not an authorization to build all LightBurn features.
